title: Physical downlink shared channel time domain resource allocation for 5g or other next generation network
abstract: Time domain resource allocation is facilitated for a physical downlink shared channel. For instance, a physical downlink shared channel associated with a mobile device can be configured via a broadcast channel, and a data structure associated with the physical downlink shared channel corresponding to a physical downlink control channel can also be configured. The configured data structure can be sent to the mobile device to be utilized for physical downlink shared channel transmissions between the wireless network device and the mobile device. Additionally, the time domain resources can be allocated by transmitting a physical downlink shared channel transmission to the mobile device based on a condition associated with a radio resource control connection being determined to not have been satisfied.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: configuring, by a wireless network device comprising a processor, via a broadcast channel, a physical downlink shared channel associated with a mobile device; configuring, by the wireless network device, a data structure associated with the physical downlink shared channel corresponding to a physical downlink control channel; sending, by the wireless network device, the data structure to the mobile device to be utilized for physical downlink shared channel transmissions between the wireless network device and the mobile device; and based on a condition associated with a radio resource control connection being determined to not have been satisfied, transmitting, by the wireless network device, a physical downlink shared channel transmission to the mobile device. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the condition associated with the radio resource control connection being determined to not have been satisfied comprises the radio resource control connection between the mobile device and the wireless network device being determined to have been established. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: receiving, by the wireless network device, an indication that the mobile device is utilizing the data structure for the physical downlink shared channel transmissions. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the physical downlink shared channel transmissions comprise symbol data representative of one or more symbols to be sent to the mobile device. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the data structure is a first data structure, and further comprising: reconfiguring, by the wireless network device, the first data structure to a second data structure different than the first data structure comprising replacing the first data structure with the second data structure to be utilized for the physical downlink shared channel transmissions between the wireless network device and the mobile device. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the data structure comprises a start value of five, representative of a sixth symbol within a slot, associated with the physical downlink shared channel. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the sixth symbol indicates a mapping type of the physical downlink shared channel. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the data structure comprises a length value of seven, the length value of seven being associated with a twelve symbol configuration for the physical downlink shared channel. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the data structure indicates to the mobile device a starting symbol associated with the physical downlink shared transmission. 10. A system, comprising: a processor; and a memory that stores executable instructions that, when executed by the processor, facilitate performance of operations, comprising: configuring, via a broadcast channel, a physical downlink shared channel associated with a mobile device; sending a first data structure to the mobile device to be utilized for a first physical downlink shared channel transmission between a wireless network device and the mobile device; in response to a condition associated with a radio resource control connection being determined to have been satisfied, configuring a second data structure, resulting in a configured data structure that corresponds to a physical downlink control channel; and sending the configured data structure to the mobile device to be utilized for a second physical downlink shared channel transmission between the wireless network device and the mobile device. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the first data structure comprises a start value of four, representative of a fifth symbol associated with the first physical downlink shared channel. 12. The system of claim 10, wherein the second data structure comprises a start value of five, representative of a sixth symbol associated with the second physical downlink shared channel. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the sixth symbol is representative of a mapping type of the physical downlink shared channel. 14. The system of claim 10, wherein the condition comprises determining that the radio resource control connection between the mobile device and the wireless network device has been made. 15. A machine-readable storage medium, comprising executable instructions that, when executed by a processor of a mobile device, facilitate performance of operations, comprising: facilitating receiving a data structure to be utilized for a first transmission associated with a first physical downlink shared channel between a wireless network device and the mobile device; facilitating establishing a radio resource control connection between the wireless network device and the mobile device; and in response to the facilitating the establishing the radio resource control connection, facilitating receiving a configured data structure to be utilized for a second transmission associated with a second physical downlink shared channel between the wireless network device and the mobile device. 16. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the operations further comprise: configuring, via a broadcast channel, the first physical downlink shared channel between the wireless network device and the mobile device. 17. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the data structure comprises a start value of four, representative of a fifth symbol associated with the first physical downlink shared channel. 18. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the configured data structure comprises a start value of five, representative of a sixth symbol associated with the second physical downlink shared channel. 19. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the data structure is a default data structure to be utilized by the mobile device in the absence of the configured data structure. 20. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the operations further comprise: utilizing the configured data structure, facilitating obtaining an allocation of a resource, associated with the configured data structure, for the mobile device.
